+++
title = "Local Groups Urge Uniting Efforts for Community Energy Projects"
date = "2026-03-05"
author = "Sarah Jenkins, Chief Reporter"
categories = ["Community"]
tags = ["Hastings","Energy Initiatives","Rye","Community Engagement"]
draft = false
+++

In a significant show of unity, various local groups in Hastings and Rye are advocating for the expansion of community-led energy projects to enhance sustainability and resilience within their communities. This initiative aims at unlocking the potential of community energy at scale, thereby promoting self-reliance and environmental stewardship.

The campaign has garnered support from environmental activists, residents' associations, and local businesses who believe that collective action can lead to substantial changes in how Hastings and Rye approach renewable energy solutions and carbon reduction goals. The groups are calling on the local council and community leaders to facilitate policies and frameworks that encourage grassroots efforts towards a greener future.

With the aim of fostering greater engagement among community members, these initiatives promise not only environmental benefits but also economic opportunities through the creation of green jobs and the development of new technologies within the renewable sector. The push for community energy projects is expected to play a pivotal role in shaping the region's response to climate challenges.

---
